children.name: Falcunculus
children.description: A genus of stocky, crested passerines endemic to Australia and New Guinea, with a unique parrot-like bill in which the upper mandible is strongly hooked and curved over the lower, giving them the appearance of a tiny shrike-tit hybrid. They use this specialised bill to strip bark from branches and to extract insects from crevices. They are deliberate, methodical foragers that move slowly through the canopy.

children.name: Falcunculus frontatus
children.description: A distinctive bird with a bold black-and-white head pattern, a tall crest, and a unique hooked bill used to strip bark like a tiny woodpecker, found in woodlands across northern and eastern Australia. It moves methodically along branches, using its bill to lever off bark and expose hidden insects. Its call is a distinctive, ringing whistle.
